_note: Issues to raise with transition2exec team.

_issues.title: Stage 1 emits tool output key names as input values — spurious keys injected into INPUTS
_issues.observed: actual inputs contain 'required_outputs(return_code): true' — output signature key bleeding into Stage 1 INPUTS resolution
_issues.root_cause: Stage 1 prompt has no rule preventing output key names from being used as input values. Model confuses tool output signature with input values when context is sparse.
_issues.fix: Add rule to Stage 1 prompt: 'Do not include tool output key names in INPUTS. INPUTS must only contain values for the tool input signature keys.'

_issues.title: Stage 1 returns status=ok with wrong tool instead of not_mappable when no tool matches
Stage 2 returns false not_mappable when abstract tool name is semantically distant — overrides a correct Stage 1 result
_issues.observed: Stage 1 tool_plan in response context correctly identifies the grounded tool and resolves inputs. Stage 2 still returns status=not_mappable with empty segments. The correct tool_plan is preserved in executable_dstt.context.tool_plan but never promoted to a segment.
_issues.pattern: Stage 1 selects tool X correctly. Abstract tool name is a domain verb that does not share tokens with the grounded tool name (e.g. 'duplicate_config' -> copy_file, 'retire_artifact' -> delete_file, 'initialize_workspace' -> make_directory). Stage 2 re-validates independently and rejects the mapping.
_issues.root_cause: Stage 2 is doing both LLM re-validation of the tool selection AND programmatic DSTT formatting. When the abstract tool name has low lexical similarity to the grounded tool name, Stage 2's LLM pass concludes not_mappable — independently and contrary to Stage 1's result. The two stages are not a pipeline; they are competing.
_issues.contract_violation: Stage 2's role is to FORMAT the executable DSTT from Stage 1's output. It is not supposed to re-validate tool selection. Stage 1 owns tool selection; Stage 2 owns formatting. Mixing the two makes Stage 2 LLM+program simultaneously, which is undefined behaviour.
_issues.ask: Stage 2 should accept Stage 1's tool_plan as authoritative when tool_plan contains a valid grounded tool name (not 'not_mappable'). Stage 2 should only return not_mappable if Stage 1's tool_plan itself says TOOL: not_mappable.
_issues.workaround: OS2IE detects the false not_mappable by checking executable_dstt.context.tool_plan. If tool_plan contains a valid TOOL name, OS2IE constructs the executable transition programmatically from the tool_plan string, bypassing Stage 2's rejection. The _healed_expected field in each affected stress seed records the correct output.

_root_cause: _select_best_match falls back to templates[0] when all scores are 0 (no name token overlap). The selected tool has zero output key overlap with the abstract transition's required outputs. No programmatic guard exists to catch this.
_fix: Add output key overlap guard in _extract_resolved_tools after _select_best_match. If selected tool's output keys have zero intersection with abstract_transition.outputs → return '(none matched)' → stage 2 produces not_mappable.
_safe_to_apply: All currently-passing seeds produce tools whose output keys match their abstract outputs — the guard will not affect them. Only seeds 06, 07, 08, 15 change: from wrong tool to not_mappable.

issues._issue: transition2exec must return not_mappable when no tool can fulfil the abstract transition
